programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Sing Sing, Little Bird
programme.desc: A romantic rendezvous is cut short by a violent home invasion, leaving young business owner Kelly Knudson bleeding to death on the floor and Lt Joe Kenda is short on leads.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Blood From A Stone
programme.desc: A bystander receives a deadly head injury during a roadside altercation, but the stakes are raised when his body disappears from the scene. When a toddler is kidnapped for ransom Kenda joins forces with an undercover team in a desperate race against time.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: We Kill the Ones We Love
programme.desc: A drive-by shooting leaves Van Terry dead; to crack the case, Lt. Joe Kenda must put his faith… in a burglar.
programme.sub-title: We Kill the Ones We Love

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Night Terrors
programme.desc: When Cheryl Brantner’s children find her dead in her bathtub, Lt. Joe Kenda must decide if this was a soapy accident or something worse.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: I Now Pronounce You Dead
programme.desc: Mother of 3 Dianne Hood is shot to death by a masked gunman on her way home from a Lupus support meeting. Joe Kenda uncovers a sordid web of lust and greed, revealing a killer no one ever expected.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Snitch
programme.desc: When officers respond to a shooting , they discover the body of a young man they know all to well -- police informant Jimmie Stevenson. it’s up to Lt. Joe Kenda to determine who pulled the trigger.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Justified
programme.desc: A young man is found gunned down, leading Lt. Joe Kenda to delve into his troubled past and his connection to the world of rare coins.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Time Bomb
programme.desc: In 1978, Lt. Joe Kenda is new to the CSPD, and gets more than he bargains for when a local bank is the target of a massive explosion.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Metal on Metal
programme.desc: A athlete is gunned down just weeks before starting college, leaving family heartbroken and detectives stumped. The man seems an unlikely target, until Lt. Joe Kenda pries into his personal life.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Death Rains Down
programme.desc: Restaurant worker Sharon Coleman is murdered in a hail of large caliber gunfire. Kenda must break through a web of lies to hunt down one of the most dangerous criminal masterminds he’s ever faced.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Settle the Score
programme.desc: When Johnny Crawford is killed in his easy chair, police are perplexed to learn he may have been shot over a musical instrument. It soon becomes clear the young man wasn’t killed for his own sins.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Blood Sisters
programme.desc: The bullet-ridden bodies of two sisters are found in a suburban house that’s littered with mysterious clues. Joe Kenda learns that the two unassuming young mothers shared an explosive secret.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: After School Special
programme.desc: Tragedy strikes when a beloved teacher is poisoned in her classroom. As doctors fight to save Barbara Lewis’ life, Lt. Joe Kenda and his team delve into her personal affairs.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Bring My Baby Home
programme.desc: A four-week-old girl is kidnapped, leaving her mother in anguish and police scrambling to find her. Joe Kenda and Robert Sapp fear she’s been sold on the black market… or worse.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Knock Knock
programme.desc: A gathering of friends turns deadly when two gunmen shoot William Davis in the head. Kenda and his team dig into his past and start to suspect someone in William’s circle is lying.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: My, My, Merry Christmas
programme.desc: Donald Ott is found shot to death, friends tell police he didn’t have an enemy in the world. But when Lt. Joe Kenda speaks with family, he learns of a relative who might hold the key to the mystery.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: I Will Hunt You Down
programme.desc: When two escaped inmates surface in Colorado Springs, it’s up to Lt. Joe Kenda and his Fugitive Unit to bring them to justice.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Run for your Life
programme.desc: A slain body of a marathon runner is found just hours before a big race. With the help of a trusted K-9 officer, Kenda sets off to find the killer himself before he strikes again.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Woof and Wheels
programme.desc: People and their dogs descended on a local PETCO to meet producers in the hopes of a session with Cesar Millan. Meet two of the problem pooches. Can Cesar dial down the bad behavior of these terrors?

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Tenacious B
programme.desc: A French Bulldog is anxious around other dogs, and a Vizsla is afraid of cars. Can Cesar help one dog improve his social skills and steer the other's behavior toward the fast lane?

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Blackjack & Memphis
programme.desc: Two pit bulls adopted together, turn on each other. Cesar is called in to bring these dueling pit bulls back together and stop their raging dog aggression.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Snickers and Maury
programme.desc: After being attacked by another dog, Snickers has become aggressive with both dogs and humans. Can previously unadoptable Maury get a second chance?

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Jonbee, Hudson & Violet, and Buford
programme.desc: A ferocious stray Jindo in desperate need of rescue, a Great Dane wreaks havoc on house and home, a boxer mourns the death of a beloved mate.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: The Red Zone
programme.desc: Two new sibling dogs fight over everything, from food to toys. Facing the possibility of giving up one of the pooches, Cesar travels south to end the civil war between the two dueling pit bulls.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Bear & Angel and Lorelei
programme.desc: Two German Shepherds develop an aggressive relationship when they begin going to day care. Then a miniature Schnauzer begins to bark and nip at her owner’s grandchildren.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: How to Raise the Perfect Dog
programme.desc: Cesar shows what it takes to raise four young puppies with different breed-related traits and how the importance of setting boundaries and rules at an early age can lead to a well behaved dog.
programme.sub-title: How to Raise the Perfect Dog

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Scrawny & Lola
programme.desc: Scrawny, one of nine Yorkies, is Allen’s favorite, but attacks anyone that comes near. Lola, a Dalmatian, barks at everything. Find out how Cesar reestablishes control in both homes.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Army Brats
programme.desc: At an army base, Cesar helps an Australian shepherd scared of men in uniform and a Shetland shepherd who barks non-stop when planes fly overhead.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Lotus, Rocco, Hootie
programme.desc: Cesar tries to restore the peace in a spiritual home after Lotus doesn’t live up to his tranquil name; A lesson on keeping dogs safe from venomous wildlife; The return of Hootie.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Made in Japan, Lawnmower Licker
programme.desc: Marley, a neglected Shiba Inu from Japan, develops aggression towards dogs and people. Piper, a Wire Fox Terrier obsessively tries to get to and lick the lawnmower engine.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Cotton, Ricky, and Lila & Duke
programme.desc: An American Eskimo watchdog who takes his role too far; a Shibu Inu with a mean streak; a boxer who was attached by another dog one day in the park, and has since become more aggressive.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Goldie & Pepsi
programme.desc: Pepsi, a German Shepherd, becomes intensely aggressive around cats. Goldie, the beautiful Brittany Spaniel of “General Hospital” star Jacklyn Zeman that has become aggressive towards female dogs.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Duke & Lulu, and Sparky
programme.desc: Cesar heads to the dog park to help some frustrated dog lovers. A therapeutic terrier helps AJ control her panic attacks but tends to attack other dogs.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: India, Jagger, and Axl
programme.desc: A Manchester Terrier attacks family members, bikes, skateboards, and visiting guests. Jagger needs to learn how to take a walk without wreaking havoc. Cesar tries to rehabilitate an ocean-phobic dog.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Baby Girl
programme.desc: Suzie Dove Miles, a "cat whisperer," is set on getting a fearful Doberman mix at a shelter. Suzie has tried many of Cesar's exercises with Baby Girl, but needs Cesar’s expertise to help Baby Girl become a real dog.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Beagle Mania, Rescue Me… Rescue You
programme.desc: At six years old, beagle Gizmo has become very aggressive. Jennifer Lee Pryor runs Pryor’s Planet, a rescue organization to honor her late husband, comedian Richard Pryor.
programme.sub-title: Beagle Mania, Rescue Me… Rescue You

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Delta and Dong Dong
programme.desc: Delta, a dog with aggressive behavior and a fear of men. Dong-Dong, a rescue from Taiwan, who has found solace in the living room chair of his adopted home. He won’t get up for anything.

programme.title: Baywatch: Countdown
programme.desc: Mitch learns that a cache of US Marine weapons has been stolen and probably hidden offshore. Mitch and Cody locate a sunken vessel with the arms on board, but before they can confiscate them, they are taken captive.

programme.title: Baywatch: Surf City
programme.desc: After breaking up with Manny, April finds romance with Craig, who is having a mid-life crisis. While working together to catch a trio of wild men on waverunners harassing swimmers, Craig and April's passion heats up.

programme.title: Baywatch: To the Max
programme.desc: Mitch uses Hobie to initiate a relationship with Hobie's horseback riding instructor. Cody, JD, and Lani try to make extra cash in a scheme to collect Sea Urchins, but when another diver gets into trouble, Lani puts her life at risk to save him.

programme.title: Baywatch: Night of the Dolphin
programme.desc: Garner enlists Mitch’s help to track down and capture a ring of drug smugglers operating from a boat offshore. The stakes get deadly when Neely goes undercover to plant a bug and her cover is blown! Lani struggles to come to terms with her deafness.

programme.title: Baywatch: Full Throttle
programme.desc: Cody makes a crazy bet with some local hotshots. Mitch risks his uncle's new Scarab in a wild race. Meanwhile, Neely and her baby daughter Ashley move in with Mitch for a couple of days sparking a twist to their relationship.

programme.title: Baywatch: Quarantine
programme.desc: After a swimmer shows symptoms of a deadly virus, headquarters is quarantined, trapping Mitch, April, Donna and JD inside with an annoying salesman. With JD waylaid, Newman takes on his plum assignment overseeing a photo shoot of sexy models.

programme.title: Baywatch: Diabolique
programme.desc: After he saves Neely's new nanny from a boating disaster, she develops a lethal attraction to Mitch. April and Manny join a charity contest arranged by Newmie for his juvenile diabetic nephew.

programme.title: Baywatch: Bon Voyage
programme.desc: While Lani prepares for a photo shoot on a cruise to Alaska, Neely travels to see her ex-husband who lives in Juneau. Hobie's good fortune lands him, Mitch and Cody on the cruise ship to Alaska as well, but danger lurks onboard.

programme.title: Baywatch: White Thunder at Glacier Bay, Part 1
programme.desc: Mitch and Cody surprise Neely and Lani by showing up on the Dawn Princess. During the stop in Juneau, Gavin finally recognizes Leslie, who takes a clueless Hobie with her in the beginning a harrowing chase…
programme.sub-title: White Thunder at Glacier Bay, Part 1

programme.title: Baywatch: White Thunder at Glacier Bay, Part 2
programme.desc: After an escalating, heart-stopping chase across a glacier, Mitch rescues Hobie and Leslie from hitman Gavin. Mitch returns to the Dawn Princess with a romantic proposal for Neely.
programme.sub-title: White Thunder at Glacier Bay, Part 2

programme.title: Baywatch: Nightmare Bay, Part 1
programme.desc: Mitch rescues an underwater photographer that appears to have been attacked by a Sea Creature in an underwater cave, resulting in a media frenzy. Shauni rescues a young girl in the ocean then has to rescue her family from drowning.

programme.title: Baywatch: Nightmare Bay, Part 2
programme.desc: When a fishing trawler mysteriously sinks, the “Creature in the Bay” is thought responsible! Mitch, Devon and Captain Thorpe set out to prove there is more than meets the eye behind this Creature's mysterious attacks.

programme.title: Baywatch: The One Who Got Away
programme.desc: After Baywatch lifeguard Megan is attacked on the beach, she struggles to regain her confidence – unaware that her attacker, a fisherman, views her as the "One That Got Away." Meanwhile, Shauni suffers from lifeguard burnout.

programme.title: Baywatch: Money, Honey
programme.desc: After Mitch makes a heroic ocean rescue while working at her private beach party, a Hollywood producer offers Mitch the starring role in a movie. Meanwhile, Harvey and Shauni hold a wild bikini contest to raise money for an aquatic rescue group.

programme.title: Baywatch: The Fabulous Buchannon Boys
programme.desc: Mitch receives an unexpected visit from his free-spirited brother, whose troubled son entices Hobie to surf the pylons of a treacherous condemned pier. Meanwhile, a local vendor nearly causes a riot when his bikinis disintegrate in the water.

programme.title: Baywatch: Point of Attack
programme.desc: After helping break up a gang fight on the beach, Eddie gets the idea of having some gang members join the lifeguard W.A.T.E.R. program designed to help troubled youths.

programme.title: Baywatch: Sandcastles
programme.desc: Baywatch lifeguards gain insight into the plight of the homeless when Hobie befriends a girl who's living on the streets. Eddie is attacked by a former mental patient known as “Hector the Collector.”

programme.title: Baywatch: Thin or Die
programme.desc: Eddie goes on a blind date with Nicole, who turns out to be morbidly overweight, which leads to complications and an unlikely friendship. But Nicole's diet pills prove life threatening... Meanwhile, Mitch’s life is comically disrupted by a stray dog.

programme.title: Baywatch: The Trophy, Part 1
programme.desc: Eddie is arrested for statutory rape after a teenage girl lies about seducing him in his tower. Meanwhile, Eric Turner, bitter about the paralyzing accident he suffered as a lifeguard, returns to Baywatch to reconcile his relationship with Megan.

programme.title: Baywatch: The Trophy, Part 2
programme.desc: Eddie is suspended from Baywatch over the statutory rape charge. He feels abandoned when no one believes his innocence pleas. Meanwhile, the reckless, paraplegic Turner, wanting to win Megan back, tries to impress her by hang-gliding off a cliff.

programme.title: Beyond Belief: Fact or Fiction: Episode 6
programme.desc: This episode features the following cases: The Dorm, The Child Artist, The Weatherman, Sit-Down Comedian, Room 245.

programme.title: Beyond Belief: Fact or Fiction: Episode 5
programme.desc: This episode features the following cases: House of Shadows, One Hand in the Till, Teasdale's Motor Car, The Vision and the Grave.

programme.title: Beyond Belief: Fact or Fiction: Episode 2
programme.desc: This episode features the following cases: Fire Station 32, The Computer, The Girl Next Door, The Wallet, The Woods.

programme.title: Drag Me to Hell
programme.desc: Christine Brown is an ambitious but kind hearted loan officer with a charming boyfriend. But when a powerful curse placed on her by the enraged pensioner, Christine is given just three days before she will apparently be literally dragged to Hell.

programme.title: Apartment 1BR
programme.desc: In this spine-chilling slice of horror, a woman discovers that her dream apartment isn’t everything she hoped for.

programme.title: Wrong Turn
programme.desc: A trip into the mountains of West Virginia for a weekend of hiking turns into a bloody horror fest.

programme.title: Dark Oracle
programme.desc: The Dark Oracle comic returns with a vengeance and the Stone twins, Cally and Lance are more at risk than ever.

programme.title: Dark Oracle
programme.desc: The comic predicts that a red sports car will get into a terrible accident and when Cally's new crush, Emmett shows up at school with the very same car, Cally is horrified.

programme.title: Dark Oracle
programme.desc: Evil Sage has taken real Sage's place and Lance and Cally are shocked when this new, uncensored Sage devastates Lance by hooking up with Vern.

programme.title: Dark Oracle
programme.desc: Lance is playing an online role-playing game 24/7 and even Dizzy thinks he's out of control. A staged intervention fails utterly and Lance is back at the keyboard.

programme.title: Dark Oracle
programme.desc: Lance and Cally find themselves the unwitting hosts of a house party that goes completely out of control.

programme.title: Dark Oracle
programme.desc: When the comic predicts that Dizzy will become a rock star, the "Dister" surprises everyone by entering a local "Canadian Idol" type contest.

programme.title: Dark Oracle
programme.desc: When Vern decides to use his expertise in the dark arts to grow his own "familiar", Cally and Lance use the comic clues to find it before the it hatches.

programme.title: Dark Oracle
programme.desc: Succumbing to temptation, Lance joins a group of older kids in a midnight graveyard game only to place himself, Cally and Sage in great danger.

programme.title: Colonel March of Scotland Yard: At Night All Cats Are Gray
programme.desc: A lady claims an odd man is following her and fears for her life. She’s later found murdered...in mismatched color, a telling clue for the inspector.

programme.title: Colonel March of Scotland Yard: The Case of the Kidnapped Poodle
programme.desc: Colonel March investigates a valuable stolen diamond as well as the kidnapping of a little boy's poodle; he thinks both incidents may be connected.

programme.title: Colonel March of Scotland Yard: The Invisible Knife
programme.desc: March must solve the mystery of how a man could be stabbed to death by an invisible knife, while attempting to summon The Devil during a black rite.

programme.title: Colonel March of Scotland Yard: The Strange Event at Roman Fall
programme.desc: A wife reports her husband, a reclusive mystery writer, has fallen off a cliff. Suspicion points to her as the killer and Colonel March investigates.

programme.title: Colonel March of Scotland Yard: The Headless Hat
programme.desc: While on holiday in France, there is a murder at the docks and Colonel March is asked by a friend of the French police to assist.

programme.title: Colonel March of Scotland Yard: The Second Mona Lisa
programme.desc: The Second Mona Lisa,' painted six months after the first, is targeted by rival collectors. Colonel March is determined to reveal the truth.

programme.title: Nosferatu
programme.desc: Vampire Count Orlok expresses interest in a new residence and real estate agent Hutter's wife.

programme.title: Deadtime Stories
programme.desc: "Bloody Mary… " It was just a silly wishing game; one that Lindsey and her friends had played a thousand times in front of a dozen different mirrors.

programme.title: Deadtime Stories
programme.desc: Peter Newman wants a brand new mountain bike. That's the grand prize for winning the school's talent competition. Now all he needs is a talent.

programme.title: Deadtime Stories
programme.desc: For Katie and Andy Lawrence, moving to a new town is painful enough. But when their parents take them on a boring sight-seeing tour, it's nothing but torture.

programme.title: Deadtime Stories
programme.desc: Everyone in Baskerville knows about Jimmy Leeds. In fact, Adam and his friends have been telling scary stories about Jimmy for years—stories they don't really believe.

programme.title: Deadtime Stories
programme.desc: Willy Tyler has the coolest train set-up ever. It takes up half of Willy’s attic and encompasses everything from medieval times to the Wild West.

programme.title: Deadtime Stories
programme.desc: For Catan Thomas and his cousin, Lea, being trapped in Bumbleweed for a family reunion and their grandfather’s 70th birthday is painful enough.

programme.title: Deadtime Stories
programme.desc: When Cody visits his grandparents at Shady Acres, he’s not expecting to find any excitement at all.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Mummy's Boy
programme.desc: Bobs mother is coming all the way from England to visit him.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Over-Exposed
programme.desc: Margaret accidentally picks up her neighbour's photos at the pharmacy.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Love's Labours Lost
programme.desc: While listening to the radio, Bob observes his charmless young assistant Penny, inspiring him to recite odes about youth and innocence.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Outward Bound
programme.desc: Bob joins his male colleagues on a canoe trip to a provincial park.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Bob's Burglary
programme.desc: While Bob and Margaret are sleeping, the local criminals, Beany and Boney clear their house - TV, video, microwave and electric toothbrush all go.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: A Patient Dies in Bob's Chair
programme.desc: When a patient dies of natural causes while in Bob's surgery, the local papers pick up the story?
programme.sub-title: A Patient Dies in Bob's Chair

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Jury Duty
programme.desc: Margaret gets called to Jury Duty but Bob has planned a holiday for them and begs her to get herself excused.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Age Before Beauty
programme.desc: Margaret gets a new hairstyle but Bob doesn't even notice.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: A Bob or Two
programme.desc: Bob buys an inexpensive painting from the neighbours at a junk sale (mostly motivated by pity since these neighbours have fallen on hard times) and hangs it in his surgery to brighten things up?

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: On Location
programme.desc: A film crew sets up shop on Bob and Margaret's street, giving them a small and unsavory taste of fame and fortune.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Stranded in Toronto
programme.desc: When Bob and Margaret take a trip to New York, their flight is re-routed to Toronto due to a storm.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: A Very Fishy Christmas
programme.desc: Bob and Margaret experience a truly Canadian Christmas.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Margaret Gets a Job
programme.desc: Bob and Margaret have made the big move to Canada.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Strangers in a Strange Land
programme.desc: Bob and Margaret are mortified to discover that while they have been cooped up at Cookie and Melvin's house in Mississauga Ontario, their dogs have been left unattended by Penny, Bob's unreliable receptionist.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Undefined Border
programme.desc: Bob and Margaret go cross-border shopping in the United States.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Fish at the Bat
programme.desc: Bob gets cajoled into joining his office softball team.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Til Death Do Us
programme.desc: Bob gets conned into buying a cemetery plot for Margaret as a surprise 15th wedding anniversary gift.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: I, Bob
programme.desc: When an efficiency expert comes to assess the Inner City Dental Clinic, Bob gets side shifted out of a job.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: The Fly on the Wall
programme.desc: Feeling belittled by his family, Bob jumps at the chance to be the subject of a fly on the wall documentary.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: A New Life
programme.desc: At a creepy chiropodist social night, Bob and Margaret encounter a couple who are rapturous about the new planned community where they now live.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Discomfort of Strangers
programme.desc: Margaret fears that her relationship with Bob is getting stale after they have a petty argument.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: The Player
programme.desc: Bob attends a personal finance seminar to help him get his hopeless financial house in order.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Mastermind
programme.desc: When Bob and Margaret have a fender-bender with a local mobster, they accidentally turn him into the police.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Party Politics
programme.desc: Margaret runs into a distant friend who asks her to have an Ann Summers-type party in her home.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: The Getaway
programme.desc: Margaret is nominated for the Golden Arch Support award for outstanding service in the field of footcare.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: No Trouble
programme.desc: This charming, witty animated series focuses on the ordinary lives of a middle class dentist and his foot doctor wife. Together, they tackle traumas as being burgled, going on a holiday and deciding whether or not having a child will ruin their lives.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Blood, Sweat and Tears
programme.desc: Bob and Margaret decide they need to get fit.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: The Holiday
programme.desc: This charming, witty animated series focuses on the ordinary lives of a middle class dentist and his foot doctor wife. Together, they tackle traumas as being burgled, going on a holiday and deciding whether or not having a child will ruin their lives.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Cottage Country
programme.desc: Bob and Margaret get invited up to the cottage of Dr. Yoselheiffer, President of the Canadian Council of Dentists.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: A Night In
programme.desc: Bob and Margaret find themselves at loose ends one evening when, to their dismay, they find that there is absolutely nothing to watch on TV.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: The Trouble with Mummy
programme.desc: Bob is concerned when he hears that his mother's best friend Pearl has passed on.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: The Wedding
programme.desc: Cookie and Melvin invite Bob and Margaret to the wedding of their goddaughter, Tiffany.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: The Cuckoo in the Nest
programme.desc: When Bobs old friend Clive, (who is a real drunken slob), is dumped by his wife, Bob lets him stay for a few days despite Margaret's objections?

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Gone to Seed
programme.desc: Bob decides to donate his sperm to a local sperm bank.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Driving Bob
programme.desc: Bob and Margaret have put it off for too long. They need to get their Canadian drivers' licenses.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: My Foot Hurts
programme.desc: Margaret gets her own local call-in radio show entitled My Foot Hurts. On the first day, nervous and short on callers, she mentions her favourite curry house on the air?

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Going Dutch
programme.desc: Margaret enters a contest and wins the grand prize: a cycling tour of Holland!

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: For Peter's Sake
programme.desc: Bob's brother Peter is a successful chef who has a cooking show producing in India.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Problems
programme.desc: At her clinic one-day, Margaret sees an old school chum, and invites her and her husband Matt to dinner.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Neighbours
programme.desc: In this story of neighbourhood social angst, Bob is having a complex burglar alarm installed by Graham, a sad, lonely man whose confidence is lifted immeasurably by this rare sale to Bob.

programme.title: On the Case: Terrifying Connections
programme.desc: When a 15-year-old boy vanished under mysterious circumstances police feared the worst. It appeared the case had terrifying connections to a serial killer who had left a trail of bloodshed across the region.

programme.title: On the Case: Crossed by Evil
programme.desc: Police race to find a young woman who disappeared on her way to work, but the clues they discover reveal a trail of unspeakable evil.

programme.title: On the Case: Notes From a Killer
programme.desc: April 1st 1988. It was a cloudy afternoon when 8-year-old April Tinsley left home for a play date. Janet Tinsley never could have imagined that would be the last conversation she would ever have with her beloved daughter.

programme.title: On the Case: Desperate Cry
programme.desc: A mother's desperate cry for help alerts neighbors and leads to a terrifying discovery.

programme.title: On the Case: A Knock on the Door
programme.desc: A beautiful young mother is found murdered in the bedroom of her home. The only witness was her five year old son. Would he be able to help police solve the crime?

programme.title: On the Case: A Hole in their Hearts
programme.desc: A mother of three disappears from her office in broad daylight, and the case remains open for eight years until two farmers unearth a clue.

programme.title: On the Case: Betrayal and Regret
programme.desc: After a teenage girl is found murdered in an isolated cemetery, police must untangle a dark mystery in order to bring her killer to justice.

programme.title: On the Case: Trail of Carnage
programme.desc: When 25 year old Laci Hill vanishes without a trace; police receive the key to solving the mystery from her kidnapper's wife.

programme.title: On the Case: Field of Nightmares
programme.desc: A young mother and son are found shot to death in a quiet Texas field. Would a mysterious bag left at the crime scene hold the key to catching their killer?

programme.title: On the Case: A Call for Justice
programme.desc: After a man confesses to a terrible double homicide, police must determine whether he actually committed the crime.

programme.title: On the Case: Fatal Footage
programme.desc: An innocent mother of two is mysteriously shot in downtown Philadelphia. Surveillance cameras record every detail but can't unmask the killer.

programme.title: On the Case: Driven to Murder
programme.desc: A woman is at first thought to be the victim a deadly hit-and-run, until police find the car that killed her nearby, and it turns out to be her own.

programme.title: On the Case: Texts, Lies, and Videotape
programme.desc: When a young mother disappears, a series of text messages from her phone lead police to believe she left on her own. Then, her family uncovers evidence that she may have actually been the victim of foul play.

programme.title: On the Case: Terrifying Calls
programme.desc: A Wilmington, North Carolina, woman is reported missing after she fails to show up for her own birthday celebration. Police never imagined that their investigation would ultimately reveal a gruesome second crime.

programme.title: On the Case: When, Where, and Who?
programme.desc: A 15-year-old girl vanishes from her front porch, and police have only a few short hours to determine who saw her last, where she was going, and who would want her dead.

programme.title: On the Case: A Blue Raincoat
programme.desc: After a young girl disappears on her way home from school, police find her blue raincoat thirty miles away. It marks the beginning of a heartbreaking homicide investigation.

programme.title: On the Case: Screams for Justice
programme.desc: The eyewitness accounts of neighbors may help or hinder investigators as they seek to solve the murder of a young woman.

programme.title: On the Case: The Disappearance of Sarah Stern
programme.desc: A car is discovered abandoned in the middle of a New Jersey bridge, with the keys inside and no driver in sight. Detectives look at three possibilities. Sarah Stern either ran way, jumped off the bridge, or was the victim of foul play.

programme.title: On the Case: A Mystery in Indio
programme.desc: When Wendi Brant was found brutally murdered in 1998, her estranged husband quickly became a person of interest. But he wasn't the only potential suspect, and this case would continue to baffle police for decades.

programme.title: Farscape: Farscape: S2 E14 - Beware Of Dog
programme.desc: The crew bring aboard an alien tracker to remove some creatures that came aboard hidden in some cargo.
programme.sub-title: Farscape: S2 E14 - Beware Of Dog

programme.title: The Dead Zone: The Storm
programme.desc: When Johnny is caught in a deadly storm, he is forced to come to terms with his own destiny in order to save those he loves.

programme.title: The Dead Zone: Plague
programme.desc: When J.J. (Spencer Achtymichuk) falls prey to a deadly new virus sweeping across the Northeast, Johnny must use his powers to solve the medical mystery in time to save his son's life.

programme.title: The Dead Zone: Deja Voodoo
programme.desc: Johnny meets a beautiful woman (Reiko Aylesworth) in danger and is reminded again of the potentially dire consequences of even the smallest of choices.

programme.title: The Dead Zone: The Hunt
programme.desc: A covert branch of U.S. intelligence recruits Johnny to track down the world's most wanted terrorist.

programme.title: The Dead Zone: The Mountain
programme.desc: After Johnny is struck by the vision of a plane lost in a crash, thieves in search of a missing treasure take Johnny, Sara, Walt and J.J. hostage.

programme.title: Starhunter Redux: Eat Sin (REDUX)
programme.desc: Under the effects of a space/time warp, members of the Tulip's crew find themselves alone in their own parallel universe, dealing with a separate version of their marauding psychopathic prisoner, "Five."

programme.title: Starhunter Redux: Bad Girls (REDUX)
programme.desc: When a wealthy socialite and his two daughters book the Tulip for a passage to Mars, Percy and the destructive young women become fast friends, creating serious trouble for Dante.

programme.title: Starhunter Redux: Bad Seed (REDUX)
programme.desc: Dante is abducted by an Orchard agent (Tosca), in an effort to track down Travis. Tosca believes that he is the key to the Divinity Cluster.

programme.title: Starhunter Redux: Travis (REDUX)
programme.desc: Dante finally finds his son, Travis, but the Raider that raised him stands between father and son reuniting, as the Earth's future hangs in the balance.

programme.title: A Century of Science Fiction: Mad Scientists
programme.desc: Take a look at the many legendary performers who have taken their turn experimenting with the formula of one of sci-fi's greatest tropes: the sinister mad scientist.

programme.title: A Century of Science Fiction: Mutations
programme.desc: From exposure to intense cosmic radiation to a grand experiment experiment gone horribly wrong, mutations have grown and developed into a key component of science fiction DNA.

programme.title: A Century of Science Fiction: Power of the Mind
programme.desc: This fascinating chapter takes a look at such mental manifestations as ESP, mind control, and telekinesis.

programme.title: Relic Hunter: Sydney at Ten
programme.desc: Sydney's roommate from school is on track of a pendant from her past and enlists Sydney to help find it and catch the killer.

programme.title: Relic Hunter: The Light of Truth
programme.desc: Arabia 843 AD: A sheik uses the Light of Truth to reveal his general as a traitor. 2001: Prince Shareem drops out from Sydney's class to go search for the Light of Truth.

programme.title: Relic Hunter: Treasure Island
programme.desc: Sydney's on the hunt for a Pirate's booty, but will a traitor force her to walk the plank?

programme.title: Relic Hunter: Women Want to Know
programme.desc: Sydney has found a temple with an idol of Ganesha and it is being filmed for TV, but they get trapped inside when the local rebels stumble onto them. Can Nigel and Karen help from USA?

programme.title: Relic Hunter: The Star of Nadir
programme.desc: An anonymous Sultan's wife Danielle dies in mysterious circumstances in the desert. However, she had called for Sydney to help. Sydney believes Danielle was searching for the Star of Nadir.

programme.title: Relic Hunter: Devil Doll
programme.desc: A guide leads Sydney and Nigel to an unusual, underground Aztec burial chamber. Instead of a body, they find a relic.

programme.title: Relic Hunter: Incognito
programme.desc: Retrieving a mythical lancet, Sydney and Nigel run into Nigel's look-alike just before his death. Nigel gets a cut from the lancet. Later, Cate persuades Nigel to impersonate the man.

programme.title: Relic Hunter: All Choked Up
programme.desc: Carson Inez, a collector, uses an ancient necklace to get Sydney to help her in raising Athena who will then help her conquer the world.

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder: Stephen Searle
programme.desc: Stephen and Anne Searle were the grandparents at the helm of a large, close-knit family. But Stephen was having an affair, and Anne had been pushed to drink by her husband’s controlling behaviour. Neither was destined to enjoy a peaceful retirement.

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder: David Clark
programme.desc: Melanie and David Clark’s marriage was the definition of toxic. He was controlling and angry, and she had affairs. They seemed unable to tear themselves apart - until one violent incident ended their marriage for good.

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder: Glyn Dix
programme.desc: Glyn Dix met Hazel while he was serving a life term for a brutal rape and murder. After convincing her he had been framed, the couple fell for one another. But five years after his release from prison, Glyn finally showed Hazel his true colours.

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder: Paul Novak
programme.desc: Paul Novak was a thrill-seeking paramedic who loved the hustle and bustle of the city. His wife Catherine was a homemaker and volunteer who preferred small town life. Unable to reconcile their differences, Paul decided to plan the perfect crime.

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder: Steven McDowell
programme.desc: Steven and Crystal McDowell were never a good match, and for years after they split, Steven deluded himself that reconciliation was just around the corner. Then, Crystal disappeared during a deadly hurricane. Was she the victim of a natural disaster?

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder: Ricardo Godinho
programme.desc: Aliny and Ricardo Godinho had four children and a relationship history that spanned 17 years. While Aliny put her kids at the centre of everything she did, for Ricardo they were a morbid obsession, and one that would lead to a shocking, brutal crime.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Peter Tobin
programme.desc: Peter Tobin is one of the most notorious serial killers in the UK. From 1991–2006 he raped and murdered three young girls; Dinah McNicol, Vicky Hamilton and Angelika Kluk. Yet even prior to these horrific murders he had a history of violence.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: John Sweeney
programme.desc: John Sweeney brutally murdered two of his girlfriends, dismembered their bodies and dumped them in canals. In a macabre twist, he hid clues in his abstract paintings, and five other girlfriends of his are currently still missing.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Manchester
programme.desc: The series returns to take a closer look at the most horrific crimes ever committed on British soil, starting with the May 2017 Manchester Arena bombing.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Ann Maguire
programme.desc: The tragic story of Ann Maguire, who was stabbed to death by a teenage pupil while teaching a Spanish lesson at Corpus Christi Catholic College in Leeds.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Gemma McCluskie
programme.desc: The harrowing story of EastEnders actress Gemma McCluskie, who was murdered by her older brother in the flat they shared in east London in 2012.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Stepping Hill
programme.desc: An inside look at the series of mysterious deaths that occurred at Stepping Hill Hospital in Stockport, Greater Manchester, in 2011.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Steven Wright
programme.desc: A closer look at the crimes of Steve Wright, who was dubbed 'The Suffolk Strangler' after murdering five women who worked as prostitutes in Ipswich in 2006.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Stephen Port
programme.desc: A chilling look at the crimes of Stephen Port, the east London serial killer who poisoned four young men with lethal doses of a date rape drug.

programme.title: Robbie Coltrane's Critical Evidence: Resolution To Kill: Colin Ireland
programme.desc: In the summer of 1993 a serial killer stalked London. The Metropolitan Police investigated a bizarre murder in which a man was found naked on his bed with a dead cat draped over him. He’d been strangled and his flat had been wiped of evidence.

programme.title: Robbie Coltrane's Critical Evidence: Little Miss Nobody: Alan Charlton
programme.desc: An old carpet dug up by workmen in a Cardiff back garden in 1989 was the beginning of a case that would push the boundaries of forensic science. The carpet contained human bones - but whose?

programme.title: Robbie Coltrane's Critical Evidence: The Perfect Murder: Roger& David Cooper
programme.desc: Robbie Coltrane examines the case of two brothers who murdered a marketing manager on Christmas Eve 2014 using chloroform.
programme.sub-title: The Perfect Murder: Roger& David Cooper

programme.title: Robbie Coltrane's Critical Evidence: Appointment With Death: Scott Singleton
programme.desc: Robbie Coltrane examines the case of Scott Singleton, who murdered a 17-year-old girl after luring her to a bogus job interview.

programme.title: Robbie Coltrane's Critical Evidence: Countryside Killings: Graham Backhouse
programme.desc: Robbie Coltrane examines why Graham Backhouse attempted to kill his wife with a car bomb before murdering his neighbour in 1984.

programme.title: Robbie Coltrane's Critical Evidence: Far From Home: Kyu Soo Kim
programme.desc: Robbie Coltrane examines the harrowing case of a London landlord who sadistically tortured and murdered two of his tenants.
programme.sub-title: Far From Home: Kyu Soo Kim

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed: Episode 11
programme.desc: In 2011, a vault of US government files about UFO sightings and alien activity was released. This series investigates these documents and re-examines key evidence about UFOs, alien abductions and government cover-ups.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed: Episode 10
programme.desc: In 2011, a vault of US government files about UFO sightings and alien activity was released. This series investigates these documents and re-examines key evidence about UFOs, alien abductions and government cover-ups.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed: Episode 9
programme.desc: We have always been told that mankind enjoys free will. Is this really true or have aliens created the means for controlling humanity?

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed: Episode 4
programme.desc: Previously sealed documents hint at compelling new theories about extraterrestrials. Could aliens be living among us? And could former US Presidents know something we don't?

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed: Episode 3
programme.desc: Experts investigate why so many UFO sightings take place in Los Angeles, determining that the answer may lie underwate

programme.title: Strangest Things: Scrab, The Ark And The Rock, The
programme.desc: Was an ancient glass scarab in Tutankhamun’s tomb created by forces that are literally out of this world? Does a 4,000-year-old tablet really have building instructions for Noah’s Ark a thousand years before the Bible is written?
programme.sub-title: Scrab, The Ark And The Rock, The

programme.title: Strangest Things: Romans, Lost Treasure And A Leech
programme.desc: What is the purpose of the strange bronze Roman dodecahedrons that have baffled archaeologists for centuries? Is a Middle Eastern metal scroll a guide to buried treasure? Is a machine powered by bloodsucking leeches the original weather app?
programme.sub-title: Romans, Lost Treasure And A Leech

programme.title: Strangest Things: The Battery, The Screaming Mummy And The Medieval Iron Hand
programme.desc: Is a 2,000-year-old weathered jar discovered on an Iraqi excavation really the world’s oldest battery? Why is a 3,000-year-old Egyptian mummy screaming? Did a metal hand from 15th century Germany belong to some kind of medieval Iron Man?
programme.sub-title: The Battery, The Screaming Mummy And The Medieval Iron Hand

programme.title: Strangest Things: The Ancient Computer, The Pigeon And The Roman Nanotechnology,
programme.desc: Is a corroded lump of bronze found on the sea floor really a 2,000-year-old computer? Was a thousand-pound World War Two bomb really piloted by a pigeon? What is the sub-atomic secret of a Roman colour-changing glass cup?
programme.sub-title: The Ancient Computer, The Pigeon And The Roman Nanotechnology,

programme.title: Strangest Things: German Codes, Egyptian Helicopters And The Future Machine
programme.desc: Is a device that looks like a typewriter in a wooden box really one of the most dangerous weapons in history? Why is there a modern helicopter carved in an ancient Egyptian temple? Can an 800-year-old Arabic machine really calculate your future?
programme.sub-title: German Codes, Egyptian Helicopters And The Future Machine

programme.title: Secrets In The Ice: Mystery Of Glacier Mummy
programme.desc: Mountain climbers in California find the body of a U.S. Army soldier. In Alaska, strange bundles of canvas and wood high up on a mountain are discovered. Volcanic glass is found in an unexpected place. A vital WWII weather station is uncovered.

programme.title: Secrets In The Ice: Siberia's Frozen Necropolis
programme.desc: Researchers find a mummy buried in a strange way. A legendary polar exploration vehicle goes missing in Antarctica. A glacier cave on Mt. St. Helens is explored. An island in the Bering Sea may contain clues to the extinction of the woolly mammoth.

programme.title: Secrets In The Ice: Secrets Of The Ice Arsenal
programme.desc: An unidentified metal object is found on Kiska Island. A centuries-old gold mining mystery is investigated. In Siberia, a network of ice tunnels is explored. Fossils unearthed in the Canadian Arctic lead to the discovery of a new species of camel.

programme.title: Secrets In The Ice: Russia's Mountain Mummy Enigma
programme.desc: Russian soldiers make a grim discovery on a mountain. An abandoned town has an unusual past. Scientists working in Antarctica find something surprising beneath the ice. In Greenland, strange depressions in the ground are investigated.

programme.title: Secrets In The Ice: Buried Secrets Of Mount Cook
programme.desc: Merging CGI recreations, advanced scanning technology and scientific research, this series journeys to the planet’s most remote, cold and inhospitable places to reveal great mysteries that were once frozen in time.

programme.title: Secrets In The Ice: The Ancient Oland Island Massacre
programme.desc: On a Swedish island, archaeologists discover a grisly site. The wreckage of an American Navy plane is located in a surprising place. Finnish farmers unearth something shocking. A baby mammoth found in the permafrost is studied.

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Sailing Stones, Brown Mountain Lights, Super Human Runner
programme.desc: ...With William Shatner. Sailing Stones, Brown Mountain Lights, Super Human Runner: A look at Death Valley boulders, mysterious dancing lights and super-marathon runners.
programme.sub-title: Sailing Stones, Brown Mountain Lights, Super Human Runner

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Time Traveller, American Stonehenge, Plant Perception
programme.desc: ...With William Shatner. Time Traveller, American Stonehenge, Plant Perception: Check out mind-reading plants, pagan human sacrifice and Californian time travellers.
programme.sub-title: Time Traveller, American Stonehenge, Plant Perception

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Lightning Strikes, Human Hibernation, Chinese Anchors
programme.desc: ...With William Shatner. Lightning Strikes, Human Hibernation, Chinese Anchors: A man survives six lightning strikes, while a toddler is frozen solid and thawed unharmed.
programme.sub-title: Lightning Strikes, Human Hibernation, Chinese Anchors

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Bullet Proof Boobs, Amazing Coincidences, Directional Cows
programme.desc: ...With William Shatner. Bullet Proof Boobs, Amazing Coincidences, Directional Cows: Cows align with the Earth's magnetic field, and a young makes an incredible connection.
programme.sub-title: Bullet Proof Boobs, Amazing Coincidences, Directional Cows

programme.title: Family Ties: All in the Neighborhood - Part 2

programme.title: Family Ties: Pilot
programme.desc: Alex escorts a pretty socialite to a dance at her country club.

programme.title: Moesha: Cold Busted
programme.desc: Mo invites Q over when she thinks the whole family is away for the day. However, they return home early and Mo and Q are caught.

programme.title: Moesha: Prom Fright
programme.desc: Mo agrees to go to another school’s prom with the son of Frank’s friend so she will no longer be busted.

programme.title: Moesha: Grandpoppa Don’t Take No Mess
programme.desc: Moesha tries to get sympathy from her visiting grandfather over having to go to a new school.

programme.title: Moesha: Labor Day Jammy
programme.desc: Mo’s new school’s annual fund-raiser conflicts with Crenshaw High’s final summer blowout “Freaknik”, and Frank and Dee insist Mo should attend the fund-raiser.

programme.title: Moesha: Day One
programme.desc: Mo attends the first day at her new school and is miserable. She has only made one friend and feels out of place.

programme.title: Moesha: Age Ain’t Nothing
programme.desc: Hakeem begins to date an older woman he meets at his store.

programme.title: Moesha: My Mom’s Not An Ottoman
programme.desc: Dee wins a free decorating consultation at Mo’s school raffle and can’t wait to redo the house.

programme.title: Moesha: The Play Scene
programme.desc: Mo writes a scene from a play set in a Blues Lounge in the 1940s for her English class, and our cast acts it out for us.

programme.title: Moesha: Use Me Once – Shame On You – Use Me Twice I’ll Kill Ya
programme.desc: Mo tells her friends at the Den that Hayley’s dad is an important record executive.
programme.sub-title: Use Me Once – Shame On You – Use Me Twice I’ll Kill Ya

programme.title: Moesha: Keepin’ It Real
programme.desc: Mo is attracted to a classmate she is working with on a project.

programme.title: Moesha: Halloween Part 1 – Kim’s Revenge
programme.desc: Mo, Kim and Niecy decide to dress as “The Women of Batman” for Halloween.

programme.title: Moesha: Break It Down
programme.desc: Frank takes Hakeem to the father/son “lock-in” at the local church, to encourage open communication and trust between fathers and sons.

programme.title: Moesha: Rhythm Dues
programme.desc: Mo encourages CPQ to submit their latest song to the local radio station for their “play it or stuff it” segment. When the song is played, it is well received. Q has written lyrics over a Morris Day song, and Morris shows up at “The Hole” where he plays.

programme.title: Moesha: The Double Date
programme.desc: Mo has been dating Bridgewood classmate Jeremy since her break up with Q.

programme.title: Moesha: He Ain’t Heavy – He’s Dee’s Brother
programme.desc: Mo gets a crush on Dee’s very attractive younger brother when he comes to visit.
programme.sub-title: He Ain’t Heavy – He’s Dee’s Brother

programme.title: Moesha: It’s My Paper And I’ll Cry If I Want To
programme.desc: Mo decides to publish her own Bridgewood newspaper when editor Mary Ellen refuses to add her to the staff of the existing paper.
programme.sub-title: It’s My Paper And I’ll Cry If I Want To

programme.title: Moesha: The “Short” Story
programme.desc: Mo’s group at the Den includes a short kid known as “Biggie”.

programme.title: Moesha: She’s Back
programme.desc: A famous female broadcast journalist who used to be friends with Frank speaks at Bridgewood, so Mo invites her to their house for dinner.

programme.title: Moesha: This Time You’ve Gone Too Far
programme.desc: Mo, Kim, Hakeem, Niecy, Morgan, Marco, and Chris head off on a road trip to San Diego.
programme.sub-title: This Time You’ve Gone Too Far

programme.title: Moesha: Body Language
programme.desc: Mo, Kim and Niecy play a game of “Truth or Dare”.

programme.title: Moesha: Model Employee
programme.desc: Rather than asking Frank every time she wants something, Mo decides to get a part-time job at the Class Act where Hakeem works.

programme.title: Moesha: Pajama Jam
programme.desc: Mo asks Frank if she can go to a slumber party at Morgan’s house.

programme.title: Moesha: Mo’s Money – Mo’s Money – Mo’s Money
programme.desc: Moesha wants to get cable and her own phone now that she has a job and is making money.
programme.sub-title: Mo’s Money – Mo’s Money – Mo’s Money

programme.title: Moesha: A House Is Not A Home
programme.desc: Moesha is living with Andell after having moved out of Frank & Dee’s home.
programme.sub-title: A House Is Not A Home

programme.title: Moesha: Moesha Meets Brandy
programme.desc: Moesha meets Brandy at a concert and we discover that Moesha is having a romance with Aaron, a college student.

programme.title: Moesha: Homecoming
programme.desc: With a little help from Myles and a visit from Grandma Mitchell, Moesha moves back home and returns to Crenshaw High.

programme.title: Moesha: Hello! What’s This?
programme.desc: Dee finds a joint in the couch while cleaning the house. Frank and Dee assume and accuse Mo, but Moesha is shocked when she catches Myles smoking.

programme.title: Moesha: Psyche Your Mind
programme.desc: Based on the family turmoil, the family begins counseling, but Moesha doesn’t want to air the family’s 'dirty laundry'.

programme.title: Moesha: Teacher
programme.desc: Moesha is attracted to a substitute teacher at Crenshaw. After they kiss, he leaves the school for 'personal reasons'.

programme.title: Moesha: I Know What You Did In The Third Grade
programme.desc: Moesha and her friends are stalked by a student who was just released from jail who they haven’t seen since the third grade when he got into trouble because of them. Parody of Brandy’s feature 'I Still Know What You Did Last Summer…'
programme.sub-title: I Know What You Did In The Third Grade

programme.title: Moesha: A Terrible Thing Happened On My Tour Of College
programme.desc: Moesha and her friends are invited to a college to attend a Prospective Freshman Weekend and someone slips a 'roofie' in her drink. Her boyfriend Aaron keeps her from being taken advantage of.
programme.sub-title: A Terrible Thing Happened On My Tour Of College

programme.title: Moesha: Birth Control
programme.desc: Moesha gets birth control pills when she decides she would like to lose her virginity to Aaron. However, things get complicated when Dee finds the pills in her purse.

programme.title: Moesha: A Class Act Christmas
programme.desc: Moesha, Hakeem, and the other employees of the Class Act are held hostage by a man dressed as Santa Claus protesting age discrimination.

programme.title: Moesha: The Crush
programme.desc: A friend of Myles who has a crush on Moesha pretends he cannot read so she can tutor him. However, after an article she writes about the success she has had with the little student is published in the LA Times, Moesha learns a hard lesson.

programme.title: Moesha: Barking Up The Wrong Tree
programme.desc: Hakeem accepts a job as assistant manager of the Class Act claiming there are financial difficulties at home. Moesha, concerned about his college education, confronts his mother who denies his claims.

programme.title: Moesha: Life Imitating Art
programme.desc: Racial tensions mount in Moesha’s high school after a mural depicting Latin American hero Caesar Chavez is purposely defaced.

programme.title: Moesha: OhMiGod, Fanatic
programme.desc: After Moesha and Kim have a huge fight, Moesha is surprised when MTV shows up at her door to film her bringing her best friend Kim to meet her favorite singer. Leanne Rimes guest stars.

programme.title: Moesha: The Rite Stuff
programme.desc: Moesha wants to boycott her debutante ball when she learns one of the girls is thrown out of the ceremony because she is pregnant.

programme.title: Royal Secrets: The Man Who Would be King
programme.desc: Prince William is seen as the 'future' of the British monarchy. The heir to the throne who surveys say most people want to see succeed his grandmother, Queen Elizabeth. Why has William become so popular?
programme.sub-title: The Man Who Would be King

programme.title: Royal Secrets: First Steps
programme.desc: Britain's royal babies used to be born and raised behind closed palace gates. Today, they are part and parcel of the commercialisation of Britain's Royal Family. Not so long ago, access to royal babies was restricted and they didn't even go to school

programme.title: Royal Secrets: An Extraordinary Queen
programme.desc: Queen Elizabeth II is the world's oldest monarch and Britain's longest serving Queen. This episode explores the secret of the woman who never expected to become a Queen.

programme.title: Royal Secrets: Edward & Mrs Simpson
programme.desc: The love affair that enthralled the world and brought down a King. The true story of the romance that cost Edward VIII his throne and defined Britain's modern Royal Family.

programme.title: Royal Secrets: Royal Wives
programme.desc: Through the ages they have come to define their Royal husbands behind the scenes. But, in the modern world the royal wives are expected to become part of the family ‘firm'. What is the secret of being a good Royal 'wife'?

programme.title: Royal Secrets: The Royal Mistress
programme.desc: Camilla Parker Bowles was famously referred to as 'the third person' in the marriage of Charles and Diana. Her long standing affair with the heir to the throne made her one of the most famous Royal mistresses in history.

programme.title: Royal Secrets: The People's Princess
programme.desc: Princess Diana became the People's Princess, but her marriage to the heir to the throne, Prince Charles, was a largely loveless affair that saw Diana fighting the royal family she had married into.

programme.title: Royal Secrets: Born to be King
programme.desc: Prince Charles is now the longest 'serving' heir to the British throne in history. He fears that he may never inherit the role he was born to. The Queen's son stands accused of interfering and meddling in British life and the politics of a nation.

programme.title: Royals And Their Pets
programme.desc: The Royal family has always had a very special relationship with their pets: from the lapdog who hid under Mary, Queen of Scots' skirts at her execution to Caesar, King Edward VII's terrier who took precedence over 9 Kings at his masters' State Funeral.

programme.title: Spying on the Royals: Part 1
programme.desc: The royal romance between Edward VIII and Wallis Simpson can now be seen through a unique new perspective: the eyes of those who spied on them, creating a secret dossier unlike any other.

programme.title: Spying on the Royals: Part 2
programme.desc: Newly declassified secret files reveal how King George VI and even the President of the United States ordered espionage on the former Edward VIII and Wallis Simpson, based on intelligence that Edward was attempting to usurp George VI.

programme.title: The Diana Story: Part I: The Young Princess
programme.desc: In the summer of 1980 a shy young girl arrives at Balmoral Castle, two years later that girl becomes a royal Princess. At just 20 years old Lady Diana Spencer married HRH Prince Charles to become HRH Princess Of Wales.

programme.title: The Diana Story: Part II: Broken Hearts
programme.desc: The marriage of Prince Charles and Princess Diana remains infamous for being one of the most turbulent royal weddings in modern history. As the cracks began to form the world watched.

programme.title: The Diana Story: Part III: Legacy of Love
programme.desc: A late-night phone call from Paris brought news that would rock the world. The sudden and tragic death of Princess Diana devastated millions. Hear what it was really like to be at the eye of the storm.

programme.title: To Diana With Love
programme.desc: A charmingly naive documentary made before the marriage of Princess Diana and Prince Charles hit the rocks looking at the young married couple as they enjoy their new life and their new young family both at home and on holiday.

programme.title: 50 Glorious Years: The Remarkable Marriage of the Queen and Prince Philip
programme.desc: Celebrating the Golden Wedding Anniversary of the Queen and Prince Philip, this programme is a sympathetic look at not only their lives together, but also an insight into Britain and the changes it has gone through in 50 Glorious Years.

programme.title: Diana: The People's Princess 20 Years On
programme.desc: Two decades after the death of Diana, Princess of Wales, the mere mention of her name is able to provoke emotion, reflection, and a flood of memories of a special person who formed a very special p...

programme.title: Mary Queen of Scots: The Red Queen
programme.desc: There are few women in history to have truly stood the test of time but the romance surrounding the ill-fated Mary Queen of Scots has ensured that her story lives on to intrigue and fascinate each new generation.

programme.title: Royal Upstairs Downstairs
programme.desc: Victoria stayed at Chatsworth House for four days in October 1932. Their host was the 6th Duke of Devonshire, one of the richest men in the land.

programme.title: Royal Upstairs Downstairs
programme.desc: Presenters Rosemary Shrager and Tim Wonnacott visit Shugborough Hall in Staffordshire following in the footsteps of Queen Victoria and her royal visits to the great and the good throughout her reign.

programme.title: Royal Upstairs Downstairs
programme.desc: Today’s visit to Harewood House in Yorkshire was made in 1835, two years before she became Queen, having learnt she would inherit the thrown four years earlier.

programme.title: Royal Upstairs Downstairs
programme.desc: Tim & Rosemary visit Holkham Hall in North Norfolk, as they retrace the steps of 16 year old Victoria on a two day visit there, in 1835, two years before she became Queen.

programme.title: Royal Upstairs Downstairs
programme.desc: Chef Rosemary Shrager and Antiques expert Tim Wonnacott visit The Royal Pavilion Brighton to reveal the story behind 19 year old Queen Victoria’s visit during the Christmas Holidays in 1838 – the year she became queen.

programme.title: Royal Upstairs Downstairs
programme.desc: Antiques expert Tim Wonnacott and chef Rosemary Shrager travel in the footsteps of Queen Victoria, our longest reigning monarch, visiting the houses, Castles and stately homes she visited throughout her life.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Exeter
programme.desc: The presenters search for properties near Exeter. Kirstie Allsopp helps a couple spend their £600,000 budget on a rural home with plenty of space and an annexe for wife Nancy's mother.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Merseyside
programme.desc: Gary Wakefield and wife Nicky don't try to disguise themselves-they admit they are the fussiest house-hunters in the country.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Warwickshire
programme.desc: It's like Doctor Dolittle meets Lawrence of Arabia as Kirstie and Phil help the camel-racing Fossett clan relocate their ever-expanding menagerie of children and wild beasts.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: South East London
programme.desc: From a design duo looking for their next project to a couple who are finally ready to put down roots and buy a home together this search takes Phil and Kirstie to South East London and on a journey like no other!

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Edinburgh Revisit
programme.desc: A year ago, Kirstie and I were pounding the streets of Edinburgh. I've come back to see if all the hard work was worth it.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: London Southside
programme.desc: Kirstie and Phil are searching in South London with three essential workers who need all the help they can get. They're all new to the area, have very modest budgets, and want to find their dream first homes.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: North Surrey
programme.desc: This week Kirstie and Phil take charge of property searches for 2 couples who hope to relocate to the leafy streets of beautiful Surrey –one of the most expensive counties in the country.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Norwich
programme.desc: This week Kirstie and Phil are called to the rescue of faint-hearted first time buyers, Helen and Paul. Scared off by serious-sounding surveys this hapless pair have loved and lost their dream home no less than three times in the last twelve months.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Midlothian
programme.desc: Professional rugby player Matt and girlfriend Amy have been giving the property market a try for over a year but so far, have failed to score.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: SW London
programme.desc: Even in the midst of the credit crunch, there are still some people who are buying, so this week Kirstie and Phil are in desirable south west London to find two very different properties.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Revisit 1: Sutton Coldfield/Norwich
programme.desc: In this episode, Kirstie and Phil are catching up with two couples who gave them two tough challenges in two fast moving property markets – Sutton Coldfield in Birmingham and Norwich.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: South Hertfordshire
programme.desc: Martine Wright and Nick Wiltshire met in 2003 and are getting married in just two months' time.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Bucks & Herts
programme.desc: Buying a house can be romantic, but if you're not careful, things can quickly turn sour.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Aberdeenshire
programme.desc: This week Kirstie and Phil are in Scotland bringing their property-finding mission to the granite city-Aberdeen. Both sets of house hunters have relocated here for work, both are looking for family homes-and both want to find them quickly.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: New England
programme.desc: This week we are with one couple who have babies on the brain and another who are only 3 months from having one.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Lincolnshire
programme.desc: With a wide range of budgets across the whole of the UK, Phil and Kirstie are challenged at every turn, and there are many!

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: North Leeds
programme.desc: From two young first-time buyers looking to settle down to a family who have been scarred by a previous purchase, this search challenges Kirstie and Phil in ways they never anticipated.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Chelmsford
programme.desc: Location, Location, Location is off to Chelmsford this week to solve the property problems of two couples at opposite ends of the budget scale.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: London Riverside
programme.desc: London is one of the most expensive and competitive cities to buy property in the world so they're going to have to pull out all the stops if they're going to make these property dreams a reality.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Welsh Borders
programme.desc: This week Kirstie and Phil are dealing with a full-blown mid-life crisis in the Welsh Borders! Management trainer Mel has set them the challenge of finding a hillside home to house his very own leadership and team building centre

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: St Albans
programme.desc: Searching in prime commuter territory where property isn't cheap. Both couples have high expectations for their first homes and our duo will need all of their property know-how to come out on top!

programme.title: Great British Menu: Banquet
programme.desc: The winners have been announced and this special documentary follows the successful chefs in the run-up to the banquet, as they make the painstaking preparations, for what is one of the most important meals they will ever cook.

programme.title: Great British Menu: North East Starter
programme.desc: It's the battle of the North East chefs – Andrew Pern, Stephanie Moon and Tim Bilton. They kick things off with their starters, but which one is going to be first off the blocks?

programme.title: Great British Menu: North East Fish
programme.desc: The chefs from the North East – Tim Bilton, Andrew Pern and Stephanie Moon pull out all the stops with their fish dishes: Seafood Stew, Fish 'Soup' with a toasted Kipper Club Sandwich and hot oak smoked Kilnsey Trout, golden tea cream, and Daleside scraps

programme.title: Great British Menu: North East Main
programme.desc: The chefs from the North East battle it out with their main courses. Stephanie Moon, Andrew Pern, and Tim Bilton are determined to win but which dish will take the poll position?

programme.title: Great British Menu: North East Dessert
programme.desc: It's the last chance for the chefs from the North East to impress as the one with the lowest total score from across the week will leave the competition today. Tim Bilton, Andrew Pern, and Stephanie Moon deliver their desserts.

programme.title: Great British Menu: North East Judging
programme.desc: It's decision time on Great British Menu for the chefs from the North East. One chef left the competition and only two remain, as they cook their entire menus for the Great British Menu judges, Prue Leith, Matthew Fort, and Oliver Peyton.

programme.title: Great British Menu: Northern Ireland Starter
programme.desc: It's the battle of the Northern Ireland chefs – Brian McCann, Chris Bell, and Chris Fearon. All of them are determined to knock out their rivals in the race to the banquet. They kick things off with their starters.

programme.title: Great British Menu: Northern Ireland Fish
programme.desc: The chefs from Northern Ireland – Chris Fearon, Chris Bell, and Brian McCann pull out all the stops with their fish dishes.

programme.title: Great British Menu: Northern Ireland Main
programme.desc: The chefs from Northern Ireland battle it out with their main courses. Chris Bell, Brian McCann, and Chris Fearon are determined to win but which dish will take the poll position?

programme.title: Great British Menu: Northern Ireland Dessert
programme.desc: It's the last chance for the chefs from Northern Ireland to impress as the one with the lowest total score from across the week will leave the competition today. Brian McCann, Chris Fearon, and Chris Bell deliver their desserts.

programme.title: Great British Menu: Northern Ireland Judging
programme.desc: It's decision time on Great British Menu for the chefs from Northern Ireland. One chef left the competition and only two remain, as they cook their entire menus for the Great British Menu judges, Prue Leith, Matthew Fort, and Oliver Peyton.

programme.title: Great British Menu: North West Starter
programme.desc: It's the battle of the North West chefs – Bruno Birkbeck, Johnnie Mountain and Lisa Allen. All of them are determined to knock out their rivals in the race to the banquet. They kick things off with their starters.

programme.title: Great British Menu: North West Fish
programme.desc: The chefs from the North West – Lisa Allen, Bruno Birkbeck, and Johnnie Mountain pull out all the stops with their fish dishes.

programme.title: Great British Menu: North West Main
programme.desc: The chefs from the North West battle it out with their main courses. Johnnie Mountain, Lisa Allen, and Bruno Birkbeck are determined to win but which dish will take the poll position?

programme.title: Great British Menu: North West Dessert
programme.desc: It's the last chance for the chefs from the North West to impress as the one with the lowest total score from across the week will leave the competition. Bruno Birkbeck, Johnnie Mountain, and Lisa Allen deliver their desserts.

programme.title: Great British Menu: North West Judging
programme.desc: It's decision time on Great British Menu for the chefs from North West. One chef left the competition and only two remain, as they cook their entire menus for the Great British Menu judges, Prue Leith, Matthew Fort, and Oliver Peyton.

programme.title: Great British Menu: Central Starter
programme.desc: It's the Central chefs battling it out – Richard Bainbridge, Aktar Islam, and Sue Ellis. Former Great British Menu champion Glynn Purnell will decide which dishes deserve the highest scores.

programme.title: Great British Menu: Central Fish
programme.desc: The chefs from the Central region– Sue Ellis, Richard Bainbridge, and Aktar Islam pull out all the stops with their fish dishes, hoping to impress former champion, Glynn Purnell.

programme.title: Great British Menu: Central Main
programme.desc: The chefs from the Central region battle it out with their main courses. Aktar Islam, Sue Ellis, and Richard Bainbridge are determined to win but which dish will take the poll position?

programme.title: Great British Menu: Central Dessert
programme.desc: It's the last chance for the chefs from the Central region to impress former champion, Glynn Purnell, as today the one with the lowest total score from across the week will leave the competition.

programme.title: Great British Menu: Central Judging
programme.desc: It's decision time on Great British Menu for the chefs from Central region. One chef left the competition and only two remain, as they cook their entire menus for the Great British Menu judges, Prue Leith, Matthew Fort, and Oliver Peyton.

programme.title: Great British Menu: South West Starter
programme.desc: It's the South West chefs battling it out – John Hooker, Paul Ainsworth, and Andre Garrett. Former Great British Menu champion Michael Caines will decide which dishes deserve the highest scores. The chefs begin with their starters.

programme.title: Great British Menu: South West Fish
programme.desc: The chefs from the South West region– Paul Ainsworth, John Hooker, and Andre Garrett pull out all the stops with their fish dishes, hoping to impress former champion, Michael Caines.

programme.desc: The chefs from the South West battle it out with their main courses. Andre Garrett, Paul Ainsworth, and John Hooker are determined to win but which dish will take the poll position?

programme.title: Great British Menu: South West Dessert
programme.desc: It's the last chance for the chefs from the South West to impress former champion Michael Caines, as today the one with the lowest total score from across the week will leave the competition.

programme.title: Great British Menu: South West Judging
programme.desc: It's decision time on Great British Menu for the chefs from South West. One chef left the competition and only two remain, as they cook their entire menus for the Great British Menu judges, Prue Leith, Matthew Fort, and Oliver Peyton.

programme.desc: It's the Scotland chefs battling it out – Michael Smith, Philip Carnegie, and Tony Singh. The chefs begin with their starters, in a bid to get their menu through to the judges tasting.
